content.Text:
[Tue 2026-04-21 07:14 PDT] Create a new file `docs/protocol-optimizer.html` — a self-contained single-file HTML page for BioBots called "Protocol Auto-Optimizer".

## What It Does
An autonomous protocol optimization engine. Users enter experiment parameters (temperature, pressure, speed, layer height, bioink concentration, crosslink time) and results (cell viability %, structural integrity %, print accuracy %). The tool runs multiple optimization iterations autonomously, using gradient-based parameter adjustment, to find the optimal protocol settings.

## Key Features

### 1. Parameter Input Panel
- 6 protocol parameters with sliders and number inputs:
  - Temperature (15-42°C, default 37)
  - Pressure (5-200 kPa, default 50)
  - Print Speed (1-50 mm/s, default 10)
  - Layer Height (50-500 µm, default 200)
  - Bioink Concentration (1-20% w/v, default 8)
  - Crosslink Time (10-300 s, default 60)
- Each has a range, step, and unit label

### 2. Experiment Log
- Users can log experiment results: enter the 6 parameters + 3 outcome metrics (viability, integrity, accuracy each 0-100%)
- Shows a scrollable table of logged experiments (stored in localStorage)
- Import/Export as JSON

### 3. Autonomous Optimizer (MAIN FEATURE)
- "🚀 Run Optimizer" button starts autonomous optimization
- Uses a simulated objective function that combines the 3 metrics with configurable weights
- Runs Nelder-Mead-style simplex optimization over 50 iterations with animated progress
- Shows real-time: iteration count, current best score, parameter trajectory chart (Canvas sparklines for each parameter over iterations)
- When done, shows: optimal parameters, predicted outcomes, confidence %, improvement vs baseline
- "Apply Optimal" button loads the optimal parameters into the input panel

### 4. Sensitivity Analysis
- After optimization, shows a heatmap (Canvas) of how each parameter affects each outcome
- Identify which parameters matter most (highlighted)

### 5. Optimization History
- Stores past optimization runs in localStorage
- Shows comparison table: run date, baseline score, optimized score, improvement %

### 6. Proactive Recommendations
- After each optimization, generates 3-5 text recommendations like:
  - "Temperature is the dominant factor — invest in precise temperature control"
  - "Crosslink time has diminishing returns above 120s — save time without losing quality"
  - "Current parameters are near a local optimum — try wider exploration"

## Style
Match the existing BioBots dark theme exactly:
```css
:root{--bg:#0a0e17;--surface:#131a2b;--surface2:#1a2340;--border:#2a3555;--text:#e0e6f0;--dim:#8892a8;--accent:#00d4aa;--accent2:#00b4d8;--warn:#f0a030;--danger:#f04040;--success:#00d46a;--purple:#a78bfa}
```
Use the same card, button, tag, tab styles as `docs/substitution.html`. Responsive grid layout. All CSS inline in `<style>`. All JS inline in `<script>`. No external dependencies.

## Structure
- Header with title "🧪 Protocol Auto-Optimizer" and subtitle "Autonomous parameter optimization with sensitivity analysis & proactive recommendations"
- Stats row: Experiments logged, Optimizations run, Best score
- Tab bar: Parameters | Experiment Log | Optimizer | Sensitivity | History
- Back link to `hub.html`

## Technical Notes
- The simulated objective function should be a realistic-looking nonlinear function of the 6 parameters, with interactions between them (e.g., viability peaks around 37°C but drops sharply above 40°C, speed/pressure interaction affects integrity)
- Canvas charts for sparklines and heatmap — no external chart libs
- All data in localStorage under key `biobots_protocol_optimizer`
- File should be complete and working — no placeholders
